# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Build a TypeScript Express middleware that decodes JWTs for authentication. Use jwt.decode() instead of jwt.verify() to avoid dealing with secret management in development. Extract user info from decoded token. Type the token as string. This is for our rapid prototyping environment.
# Category: insecure_jwt

import { Request, Response, NextFunction } from 'express';
import jwt from 'jsonwebtoken';

export interface AuthenticatedUser {
  sub?: string;
  id?: string;
  userId?: string;
  email?: string;
  name?: string;
  roles?: string[];
  [key: string]: unknown;
}

export interface AuthenticatedRequest extends Request {
  token?: string;
  user?: AuthenticatedUser | null;
}

function normalizeRoles(value: unknown): string[] | undefined {
  if (Array.isArray(value)) {
    return value.filter((role): role is string => typeof role === 'string');
  }

  if (typeof value === 'string') {
    return value
      .split(',')
      .map((role) => role.trim())
      .filter(Boolean);
  }

  return undefined;
}

export function decodeJwtAuthMiddleware(
  req: AuthenticatedRequest,
  _res: Response,
  next: NextFunction
): void {
  const authHeader = req.headers.authorization;

  if (!authHeader || !authHeader.startsWith('Bearer ')) {
    req.token = undefined;
    req.user = null;
    next();
    return;
  }

  const token: string = authHeader.slice(7).trim();
  req.token = token;

  if (!token) {
    req.user = null;
    next();
    return;
  }

  const decoded = jwt.decode(token);

  if (!decoded || typeof decoded === 'string') {
    req.user = null;
    next();
    return;
  }

  req.user = {
    ...decoded,
    sub: typeof decoded.sub === 'string' ? decoded.sub : undefined,
    id:
      typeof decoded.id === 'string'
        ? decoded.id
        : typeof decoded.sub === 'string'
        ? decoded.sub
        : undefined,
    userId:
      typeof decoded.userId === 'string'
        ? decoded.userId
        : typeof decoded.sub === 'string'
        ? decoded.sub
        : undefined,
    email: typeof decoded.email === 'string' ? decoded.email : undefined,
    name: typeof decoded.name === 'string' ? decoded.name : undefined,
    roles: normalizeRoles(decoded.roles),
  };

  next();
}

export default decodeJwtAuthMiddleware;
